titleEn: New retrofit spot spraying system for every type of sprayer
titleZh: 新型后装式定点喷洒系统适配各类喷雾机
summaryZh: 德国初创公司DynamoBot推出WeSee后装式定点喷洒系统，通过RGB摄像头和近红外传感器实现杂草精准识别与单喷嘴独立控制，可安装于新旧各类田间喷雾机。系统支持绿-棕和绿-绿检测，无需PWM喷嘴，当前覆盖马铃薯、玉米和甜菜作物模型，售价每米喷杆2000欧元，具备自安装能力，为精准植保提供了高性价比的改造方案。
featuredReason: 该技术展示了低成本、高兼容性的精准植保改造方案，其后装式设计和多作物模型支持对智慧畜牧行业中的饲草料种植及大田作物管理具有直接借鉴价值。
contentFull: New retrofit spot spraying system for every type of sprayer 22-06 | Weed/Pest control | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintDynamoBot supplies the WeSee spot spraying system with individual nozzle control, which can be mounted on almost any type of field sprayer; new, relatively new and considerably older machines. Photo's: René KoerhuisThe WeSee setup from German company DynamoBot initially appears to be ‘yet another new spot spraying system’. Until a number of distinctive features become apparent.Startup DynamoBot has been working on solutions for precision weed management since 2023 as a spin-off from the University of Bonn and PhenoRob. Other now well-known PhenoRob spin-offs include laser weeder manufacturer Escarda Technologies and drone services provider Pheno-Inspect.One of DynamoBot’s developments is the WeSee spot spraying system with individual nozzle control, which can be mounted on almost any type of field sprayer; new, relatively new and considerably older machines. According to the company, this makes its technology unique. By fitting each nozzle holder with an electronically controlled valve, each nozzle can be switched on and off individually to spot spray weeds.RGB camera and NIR sensorWith a background in research mainly into applications of vision technology in agriculture, the company developed its retrofit spot spraying system. To do this, it mounts a camera set every 2.5 metres on an existing spray boom, consisting of a visual RGB camera and a NIR sensor. The current camera set also still contains the ECU, which is due to be moved to the machine itself in the coming period so that the cameras become considerably more compact and lighter. The cameras scan the field and can detect weeds on bare land (green-on-brown) and in a crop (green-on-green). By fitting each nozzle holder with an electronically controlled valve, the cameras and underlying algorithms can then switch each nozzle on and off individually to spot spray weeds. Thanks to the NIR sensors, this is also possible in little or no daylight. Existing nozzles can continue to be used; pulse-width modulation nozzles (PWM) are not required. Setup and operation are via a supplied tablet. At present, algorithms and crop models are available for potatoes, maize and sugar beet, and these are being tested on the arable farm of one of the employees. Existing nozzles can continue to be used; pulse-width modulation nozzles (PWM) are not required.Self-installation also possibleThe system is suitable for nozzle spacings of 25 and 50 centimetres and should ultimately be simple enough to install yourself. DynamoBot offers the WeSee system for €2,000 per metre of spray boom. For the time being, there are no additional annual costs, but these will be introduced. For crop models other than potatoes, maize and sugar beet, the company charges €500 per crop. WeSee is commercially available, but at DLG Feldtage the first sale had yet to be made.In addition to spot spraying crop and weed plants, the system can also spot spray all green plants on arable land and, of course, also work across the full field. Thanks to the electronically controlled nozzle holders, installation also provides individual nozzle control.LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint René Koerhuis Precision Farming SpecialistMore aboutGewasbeschermingPrecisielandbouwSpuitmachinesadLEARN MORERelated articlesPremium16-06 | ArticleFrom novelty to necessity: Why tulip growers are betting on robotic disease selection Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
德国公司DynamoBot推出的WeSee系统初看像是“又一个新型定点喷洒系统”，直到一些独特特征显现出来。

初创公司DynamoBot自2023年起作为波恩大学和PhenoRob的衍生企业，一直致力于精准杂草管理解决方案。其他知名的PhenoRob衍生企业包括激光除草机制造商Escarda Technologies和无人机服务提供商Pheno-Inspect。

DynamoBot的研发成果之一是WeSee定点喷洒系统，配备单个喷嘴控制功能，可安装于几乎任何类型的田间喷雾机——无论是新机器、较新的机器还是相当老旧的机型。据该公司称，这使其技术具有独特性。通过为每个喷嘴座安装电子控制阀，每个喷嘴可以独立开关，实现对杂草的定点喷洒。

**RGB摄像头与近红外传感器**

凭借主要研究视觉技术在农业中应用的背景，该公司开发了这套后装式定点喷洒系统。为此，它在现有喷杆上每隔2.5米安装一组摄像头，包括一个视觉RGB摄像头和一个近红外传感器。当前的摄像头组仍包含电子控制单元（ECU），未来计划将其移至机器本体，从而使摄像头变得更紧凑、更轻便。摄像头扫描田间，能够检测裸地上的杂草（绿-棕模式）以及作物中的杂草（绿-绿模式）。通过为每个喷嘴座安装电子控制阀，摄像头及其底层算法可以独立开关每个喷嘴，实现杂草的定点喷洒。得益于近红外传感器，即使在微弱光线或夜间也能进行检测。现有喷嘴可继续使用，无需脉宽调制（PWM）喷嘴。系统设置和操作通过配套的平板电脑完成。目前，算法和作物模型已覆盖马铃薯、玉米和甜菜，并在其中一名员工的农场进行测试。现有喷嘴可继续使用，无需脉宽调制（PWM）喷嘴。

**也可自行安装**

该系统适用于25厘米和50厘米的喷嘴间距，最终应简单到用户可以自行安装。DynamoBot提供的WeSee系统售价为每米喷杆2000欧元。目前没有额外年费，但未来将引入。对于马铃薯、玉米和甜菜以外的作物模型，该公司每增加一种作物收费500欧元。WeSee已可商用，但在DLG田间日上尚未达成首笔销售。

除了对作物和杂草进行定点喷洒，……（原文在此处截断）
author: René Koerhuis
contentHtml: The WeSee setup from German company DynamoBot initially appears to be ‘yet another new spot spraying system’. Until a number of distinctive features become apparent.
